{"step_by_step_thinking": "To determine the mechanism of chloroquine-associated retinal toxicity, I reviewed the provided documents. Document [3] from MEDCORP explicitly describes the mechanism: chloroquine and hydroxychloroquine bind to melanin in the retinal pigment epithelium (RPE), inhibit lysosomal activity, and reduce phagocytosis of shed photoreceptor outer segments. This leads to accumulation of material, triggering a response where pigment-containing RPE cells migrate into the outer nuclear and outer plexiform layers of the retina, resulting in irreversible photoreceptor loss and RPE atrophy. This is the most specific and detailed explanation of the pathophysiological mechanism. Other documents describe clinical manifestations (e.g., SD-OCT findings, FAF changes, visual field defects) or related conditions (e.g., differential diagnosis with age-related macular degeneration), but do not explain the underlying mechanism. The UMLS documents are more general and do not provide a specific mechanism for chloroquine retinal toxicity. Therefore, the mechanism described in Document [3] is the most accurate and directly relevant.", "answer_choice": "A"}
